{"content": "     1|# Skill — slack-org-wiki
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`slack-org-wiki` is the workspace-local Slack source lane for **official Slack CLI auth**, **Enterprise admin conversation inventory**, and **Slack ingestion into local wiki surfaces**.
     6|
     7|Use it when the user wants to:
     8|- bootstrap Slack admin access via the official Slack CLI
     9|- inspect org-wide accessible channels/conversations
    10|- turn recent Slack findings into an LLM-maintained company wiki
    11|- keep the current bot runtime while adding a separate admin/control lane
    12|
    13|## When to Use
    14|
    15|Route here for prompts like:
    16|- “Log me into Slack CLI and use my admin access.”
    17|- “Inventory every accessible Slack channel for this org.”
    18|- “Turn recent Slack chatter into an LLM wiki.”
    19|- “Use the official Slack CLI path, not pasted tokens.”
    20|- “Build a company memory/wiki from Slack without storing raw messages.”
    21|
    22|If the request is only about querying already-built wiki pages, prefer `company-wiki` or `slack-llm-wiki-retrieval`, both of which now route to the canonical `workspace/company-wiki/` surface.
    23|
    24|## Core Rules
    25|
    26|- Default to the assistant's configured Slack bot token/permissions when describing or executing Slack access in this workspace.
    27|- Treat Slack CLI user-auth as a deprecated/legacy fallback only for diagnosis, not the default operating story.
    28|- For company-wiki refresh and Slack metadata/history capture, prefer the bot-token path end-to-end.
    29|- Preserve the existing Socket Mode bot/runtime.
    30|- Do not paste auth tickets, challenge codes, or tokens into chat.
    31|- Do not put raw Slack transcripts, raw message bodies, or raw file bodies into derived wiki pages.
    32|- Raw Slack snapshots are allowed only under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/` when using the unified `company-wiki` pipeline.
    33|- Distinguish clearly between:
    34|  1. CLI auth/bootstrap
    35|  2. conversation metadata inventory
    36|  3. ephemeral search/review
    37|  4. persistent LLM-wiki storage
    38|- Do not claim private-channel message visibility unless the specific Slack API path and permissions are actually available.
    39|- Treat Real-time Search results as non-persistable.
    40|- Require proper internal disclosure/consent/privacy review before storing derived Slack knowledge.
    41|- Do not position this workflow as covert surveillance; align it with legitimate admin/compliance/internal-knowledge use.
    42|
    43|## Local Helper Scripts
    44|
    45|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/auth_status.py`
    46|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py`
    47|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/recent_public_channels.py`
    48|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py`
    49|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py`
    50|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py`
    51|
    52|Reference docs:
    53|- `workspace/docs/slack-org-wiki/official-notes.md`
    54|- `workspace/docs/slack-org-wiki/llm-wiki-schema.md`
    55|
    56|## Workflows
    57|
    58|### 1. Confirm bot-token/auth state first
    59|
    60|```bash
    61|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/auth_status.py --format markdown
    62|```
    63|
    64|Use this first to confirm:
    65|- local Slack helper/tooling state
    66|- whether the configured bot/service token path is usable
    67|- whether legacy `~/.slack/credentials.json` exists only as a fallback/diagnostic artifact
    68|
    69|### 2. Inventory accessible conversations (metadata only, bot-token default)
    70|
    71|```bash
    72|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    73|  --token "$SLACK_BOT_TOKEN" \
    74|  --method conversations-list \
    75|  --channel-type public_channel \
    76|  --exclude-archived \
    77|  --format markdown
    78|```
    79|
    80|for the current directly accessible public-channel surface, or:
    81|
    82|```bash
    83|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    84|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
    85|  --method oversight \
    86|  --format markdown
    87|```
    88|
    89|or, with an explicit token:
    90|
    91|```bash
    92|SLACK_ADMIN_TOKEN=*** \
    93|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    94|  --method oversight \
    95|  --format markdown
    96|```
    97|
    98|Use this for org/workspace conversation cataloging. Output is metadata-only.
    99|
   100|### 3. Join visible public channels with the oracle bot when history access is needed
   101|
   102|```bash
   103|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py --all-public
   104|```
   105|
   106|Use this when the bot token has history scopes but is not yet a member of target public channels. Joining may convert `not_in_channel` into usable history access.
   107|
   108|### 4. Build Slack-derived pages into the canonical company wiki
   109|
   110|```bash
   111|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py \
   112|  --input workspace/tmp/slack-summary.json \
   113|  --root workspace/company-wiki
   114|```
   115|
   116|Input must already be a **structured summary artifact**. Do not feed raw transcripts.
   117|
   118|### 5. Pull active public-channel candidates for manual/LLM summarization
   119|
   120|```bash
   121|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/recent_public_channels.py \
   122|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
   123|  --days 7 \
   124|  --format markdown
   125|```
   126|
   127|This is a **channel-activity heuristic** based on channel metadata, not proof of recent message reads.
   128|
   129|### 6. Query the canonical company wiki
   130|
   131|```bash
   132|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   133|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   134|  --query "product risk"
   135|```
   136|
   137|## Output Contract
   138|
   139|Default structure:
   140|1. **Auth / Access State**
   141|2. **Inventory Findings**
   142|3. **Wiki Findings**
   143|4. **Caveats / Permission Limits**
   144|5. **Next Actions**
   145|
   146|When the user asks what Slack access is currently possible, explicitly separate:
   147|- **Direct chat/thread surface**: what Orbiracle can read/respond to in the current Slack conversation itself
   148|- **Backend Slack retrieval surface**: what local Slack API/CLI tooling can inventory, join, or capture outside the current thread
   149|
   150|Do not collapse those into a single generic "limited access" statement. If public-channel metadata/history is available through the backend lane, say that clearly even if the live chat surface itself is thread-bounded.
   151|
   152|## Guardrails
   153|
   154|- Never store raw Slack messages in generated wiki artifacts.
   155|- If raw Slack data is persisted for the unified company-wiki pipeline, keep it under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/` with run-manifest visibility.
   156|- Slack wiki build/query wrappers are canonical-root adapters; they must not create a parallel Slack-only wiki root.
   157|- Never imply that Slack CLI auth alone grants unrestricted private-channel message access.
   158|- Prefer metadata inventory plus structured summaries over bulk collection.
   159|- Current public-channel “active in last 7 days” flow is heuristic unless `channels:history` or `search:read` is added later.
   160|- If the required admin/oversight scopes are missing, say so explicitly.
   161|", "total_lines": 160, "file_size": 6457, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — create_slack_cron
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`create_slack_cron` creates **reliable Slack-delivery cron automations** using a
     6|shell wrapper instead of agent cron `delivery.announce`.
     7|
     8|Use this when the output must actually land in Slack on schedule and the safer
     9|path is:
    10|
    11|1. shell cron starts
    12|2. local script runs `zeroclaw agent -m "<prompt>"`
    13|3. output is saved to disk
    14|4. `zeroclaw channel send` posts it to Slack
    15|
    16|## When to Use
    17|
    18|Use for prompts like:
    19|
    20|- “슬랙으로 가는 크론 만들어줘”
    21|- “매일/매주 보고를 Slack에 자동 발송해줘”
    22|- “cron delivery 말고 직접 Slack 보내는 방식으로 붙여줘”
    23|- “문제 있는 announce cron 말고 우회 경로로 자동화해줘”
    24|
    25|## Standard Build Pattern
    26|
    27|For each Slack cron, create:
    28|
    29|1. `workspace/prompts/<name>.prompt.txt`
    30|2. `workspace/bin/send_<name>.sh`
    31|3. a shell cron job that calls that script
    32|
    33|## Script Contract
    34|
    35|The shell script should usually:
    36|
    37|1. set `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`
    38|2. read a prompt file
    39|3. run:
    40|   - `cd "$ROOT" && RUST_LOG=error zeroclaw agent -m "$prompt"`
    41|4. save stdout to:
    42|   - `workspace/reports/` for report-style outputs
    43|   - `workspace/alerts/` for alert-style outputs
    44|5. save stderr to `workspace/logs/`
    45|6. send stdout with:
    46|   - `zeroclaw channel send "$text" --channel-id slack --recipient <CHANNEL_ID>`
    47|
    48|## Naming Convention
    49|
    50|- Prompt file: `workspace/prompts/<slug>.prompt.txt`
    51|- Script file: `workspace/bin/send_<slug>.sh`
    52|- Report file: `workspace/reports/<slug>-<date>.md`
    53|- Alert file: `workspace/alerts/<slug>-<timestamp>.md`
    54|
    55|## Cron Convention
    56|
    57|- Prefer **shell cron** over agent cron for Slack delivery.
    58|- If an older agent cron already exists for the same purpose, pause it.
    59|- Default timezone to `UTC` unless the user requests another timezone.
    60|- After creation, test the script once manually if safe.
    61|
    62|## Output Checklist
    63|
    64|When finishing, report:
    65|
    66|1. created prompt path
    67|2. created script path
    68|3. cron job id
    69|4. schedule + timezone
    70|5. whether a live send test succeeded
    71|
    72|## Guardrails
    73|
    74|- Do not claim Slack delivery is working unless `zeroclaw channel send` or the
    75|  wrapper script was actually tested successfully.
    76|- Keep prompt text outside the shell script when possible.
    77|- Prefer concise, operator-usable Slack outputs.
    78|", "total_lines": 77, "file_size": 2268, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|#!/bin/zsh
     2|set -euo pipefail
     3|
     4|ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"
     5|LOG="$ROOT/workspace/logs/company-wiki-refresh-cron.log"
     6|SCHEDULE="${COMPANY_WIKI_CRON_SCHEDULE:-17 * * * *}"
     7|TEAM="${COMPANY_WIKI_SLACK_TEAM:-moveis}"
     8|ARCHIVE_MODE="${COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE:-incremental}"
     9|NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE="${COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE:-25}"
    10|NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE="${COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE:-10}"
    11|LINE="$SCHEDULE COMPANY_WIKI_SLACK_TEAM=$TEAM COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE=$ARCHIVE_MODE COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE=$NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE=$NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE $ROOT/workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh >> $LOG 2>&1"
    12|
    13|TMP=$(mktemp)
    14|trap 'rm -f "$TMP"' EXIT
    15|
    16|crontab -l > "$TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
    17|grep -Fv "$ROOT/workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh" "$TMP" > "${TMP}.next" || true
    18|mv "${TMP}.next" "$TMP"
    19|echo "$LINE" >> "$TMP"
    20|crontab "$TMP"
    21|crontab -l
    22|", "total_lines": 21, "file_size": 946, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Company Wiki Schema
     2|
     3|## Canonical root
     4|- `workspace/company-wiki`
     5|
     6|## Authority
     7|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
     8|- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.
     9|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
    10|
    11|## Reading order
    12|1. `queries/` — latest executive memo, realignment note, or retained synthesis first
    13|2. `concepts/` — source-overview pages that explain what Slack/Trello signals currently mean
    14|3. `entities/` — channel, board, repo, or other subject-level drill-down only after the executive/context pages
    15|4. `comparisons/` — explicit tradeoff pages when multiple options or surfaces are being evaluated side-by-side
    16|
    17|## Coverage model
    18|- Auto-built canonical pages currently come from normalized Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi + Notion snapshots.
    19|- Repo skills/docs and cron/runtime state remain valid orientation sources for manual wiki audits even when they are not yet auto-ingested into normalized entries.
    20|- Notion raw exports should preserve workspace inventory, data-source row snapshots, and selected first-level page-content captures under `raw/notion/`.
    21|- Derived pages must keep provenance to manifests, source refs, or clearly named live evidence.
    22|- Raw source bodies stay under `raw/`; derived pages should stay summary-first.
    23|
    24|## Layout
    25|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots, imported history, and manifests
    26|- `entities/` — durable subjects
    27|- `concepts/` — durable operating knowledge
    28|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
    29|- `queries/` — retained syntheses and fallback classification bucket
    30|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized inputs, cutover metadata
    31|
    32|## Classification fallback
    33|- If an entry does not fit `entities/`, `concepts/`, or `comparisons/`, it lands in `queries/`.
    34|", "total_lines": 33, "file_size": 1825, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|#!/bin/zsh
     2|set -euo pipefail
     3|
     4|ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"
     5|SLACK_TEAM="${COMPANY_WIKI_SLACK_TEAM:-moveis}"
     6|ARCHIVE_MODE="${COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE:-incremental}"
     7|NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE="${COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE:-25}"
     8|NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE="${COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE:-10}"
     9|
    10|cd "$ROOT"
    11|if [ -f "$HOME/.hermes/.env" ]; then
    12|  set -a
    13|  source "$HOME/.hermes/.env"
    14|  set +a
    15|fi
    16|PYTHONPATH="$ROOT/workspace/bin/company-wiki${PYTHONPATH:+:$PYTHONPATH}" \
    17|  python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py \
    18|    --slack-team "$SLACK_TEAM" \
    19|    --archive-mode "$ARCHIVE_MODE" \
    20|    --notion-page-batch-size "$NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE" \
    21|    --notion-data-source-batch-size "$NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE"
    22|", "total_lines": 21, "file_size": 737, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

    23|  - live: Orbi trending searches snapshot (2026-04-13)
    24|---
    25|
    26|# 2026-04-13 initial wiki reordering
    27|
    28|## Situation
    29|초기 위키는 Slack/Trello 수집은 붙어 있는데, 첫 화면이 아직 **엔티티 나열형**이다. 운영 리드가 바로 읽어야 할 순서는 엔티티가 아니라 **판단 메모 → 소스 개요 → 개별 대상**이다.
    30|
    31|## Evidence snapshot
    32|- Slack 최신 run manifest 기준 public channels 7개 join 성공, history capture 성공 7개, chunk 저장은 `#서비스-배포-알림`, `#ai_news`에서 확인된다.
    33|- Trello 최신 manifest 기준 visible workspace 1개, open boards 4개, collection_count 6이다.
    34|- GitHub live surface는 정상 인증돼 있고 최근 업데이트 상위 repo는 `orbi-oracle-bot`, `ai-math`, `tutor`, `orbi-viewer-mcp`, `spica` 순으로 보인다.
    35|- Orbi live surface는 hot posts와 trending searches가 현재 응답한다. 반면 MCP generic list endpoints는 이 브리지에서 직접 노출되지 않았다.
    36|- 시스템 crontab에는 `17 * * * * ... run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`가 살아 있다.
    37|- skill/router 기준 회사 위키 해석면은 `company-wiki`, Slack 수집 lane은 `slack-org-wiki`, GitHub 실행면은 `github-org-intel`, Trello exec 해석면은 `trello-wiki-exec-report`, 자동화 문맥은 `create_slack_cron`이 담당한다.
    38|
    39|## Analysis
    40|### 1. 첫 화면은 executive-first가 맞다
    41|대표/운영 리드가 처음 봐야 하는 건 “무슨 소스가 살아 있고”, “무엇이 자동 반영되고”, “어디까지가 아직 수동 해석 구간인지”다.
    42|
    43|### 2. canonical auto-ingest와 live-access surface를 분리해서 보여줘야 한다
    44|현재 canonical auto-ingest는 Slack+Trello다. 하지만 실제 운영상 사용 가능한 리소스는 GitHub, Orbi, skill/docs, cron까지 넓다. 이 차이를 인덱스와 스키마에서 명시해야 오해가 줄어든다.
    45|
    46|### 3. 위키 hygiene는 바로잡아야 했다
    47|빌더가 `entities -> entitie`로 잘못 단수화하고 있었고, 인덱스도 단순 section dump였다. 이건 검색 품질과 신뢰를 같이 깎는다.
    48|
    49|## Reordered reading path
    50|1. `queries/` — 최신 판단 메모
    51|2. `concepts/` — Slack/Trello source overview
    52|3. `entities/` — channel/board drill-down
    53|4. `comparisons/` — 선택지 비교가 생길 때 추가
    54|
    55|## Recommendation
    56|- company wiki의 기본 독서 순서를 executive-first로 고정한다.
    57|- 인덱스에 Slack/Trello auto-ingest, GitHub/Orbi live surface, skill/docs/cron context를 분리 표기한다.
    58|- 수동 판단 메모는 보존 대상으로 취급해서 다음 빌드에도 살아남게 해야 한다.
    59|
    60|## Result applied in this cycle
    61|- `wiki_build.py`에서 `type: entity` 단수화 버그를 수정했다.
    62|- `SCHEMA.md`에 reading order와 coverage model을 추가했다.
    63|- `index.md`를 executive-first landing 구조로 재작성했다.
    64|- 수동 query/comparison 페이지가 빌드 시 사라지지 않도록 preservation 로직을 추가했다.
    65|", "total_lines": 64, "file_size": 3867, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

     8|  - workspace/company-wiki/concepts/notion-workspace-overview.md
     9|  - workspace/company-wiki/entities/notion-root-page-home.md
    10|  - workspace/bin/company-wiki/notion_snapshot.py
    11|  - workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py
    12|  - workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_build.py
    13|  - workspace/skills/company-wiki.md
    14|---
    15|
    16|# 2026-04-14 notion canonical integration
    17|
    18|## Situation
    19|노션 워크스페이스가 canonical company-wiki 바깥의 별도 문서창구로 남아 있으면, 운영 판단면이 Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi와 분리된다. 지금 필요한 건 “노션도 같은 canonical 루트에서 raw → normalized → wiki” 흐름으로 붙는 상태다.
    20|
    21|## Evidence snapshot
    22|- 최신 top-level run manifest `20260414T013416Z.json`에서 `sources.notion.status = ok`가 확인된다.
    23|- 최신 Notion source manifest `20260414T013454Z.json` 기준 accessible snapshot은 `717 pages`, `20 data sources`, `created_count 556`, `deduped_count 315`다.
    24|- raw Notion store에는 현재 `workspace_search_catalog`, `page_record`, `page_children_recursive`, `data_source_record`, `data_source_query` 5개 collection이 남는다.
    25|- canonical wiki에는 `concepts/notion-workspace-overview.md`, `entities/notion-root-page-home.md`, 그리고 14개 이상의 Notion data-source entity 페이지가 생성되었다.
    26|- raw item count는 현재 1,521개다. 즉 이번 초기 적재는 단순 키 등록 수준이 아니라 실제 raw inventory/cache가 형성된 상태다.
    27|
    28|## Analysis
    29|### 1. 노션이 이제 canonical substrate에 들어왔다
    30|이번 변경으로 노션은 ad-hoc 조회가 아니라, `refresh.py`의 정식 source adapter로 편입됐다. 즉 다음 리프레시부터도 같은 회사 위키 루트 아래에서 반복 갱신된다.
    31|
    32|### 2. 이번 초기 raw 적재는 “전량 인벤토리 + 데이터소스 row snapshot + 비데이터베이스 페이지 content capture”로 보는 게 정확하다
    33|717개 페이지 전부에 대해 metadata inventory는 남았고, 20개 데이터소스는 row-level query snapshot이 남았다. 페이지 content는 비용 대비 효율을 위해 non-database page 중심의 first-level block capture로 남겼다. 이것만으로도 초기 canonical indexing 기준선으로는 충분히 강하다.
    34|
    35|### 3. 운영상 읽는 순서도 맞게 붙었다
    36|index 첫 화면에 Notion workspace overview가 concept layer로 올라왔다. 즉 노션은 raw dump만 생긴 게 아니라, 실제 경영/운영 읽기 순서에 맞는 overview surface까지 확보됐다.
    37|
    38|## Recommendation
    39|- 앞으로 company-wiki refresh는 노션을 기본 source로 간주한다.
    40|- 노션 deep-dive가 필요한 영역은 data-source별 executive memo를 `queries/`에 추가하는 방식으로 확장한다.
    41|- 현재 raw/notion은 baseline이 생겼으니, 다음 단계는 PRD/회의록/Todo처럼 row count가 큰 data source부터 management-facing retained memo를 붙이는 것이다.
    42|
    43|## Result applied in this cycle
    44|- `workspace/bin/company-wiki/notion_snapshot.py` 추가
    45|- `workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py`에 Notion source adapter 연결
    46|- `workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_build.py`에 Notion classification/coverage/index 반영
    47|- `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`에 Notion canonical adapter 문서화
    48|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/notion/`에 초기 raw inventory/cache 적재 완료
    49|", "total_lines": 48, "file_size": 3572, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2|from __future__ import annotations
     3|
     4|import argparse
     5|import json
     6|import shutil
     7|import subprocess
     8|import sys
     9|from pathlib import Path
    10|from typing import Any
    11|
    12|import slack_snapshot
    13|import trello_snapshot
    14|import github_snapshot
    15|import orbi_snapshot
    16|import notion_snapshot
    17|from _common import (
    18|    COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT,
    19|    LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT,
    20|    CompanyWikiError,
    21|    append_contract_log,
    22|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root,
    23|    company_wiki_paths,
    24|    ensure_contract_ready,
    25|    now_iso,
    26|    write_json,
    27|)
    28|
    29|ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
    30|TMP_ROOT = ROOT / 'workspace' / 'tmp'
    31|
    32|
    33|def copy_tree_contents(src: Path, dest: Path) -> None:
    34|    dest.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    35|    for item in src.iterdir():
    36|        target = dest / item.name
    37|        if target.exists():
    38|            continue
    39|        if item.is_dir():
    40|            shutil.copytree(item, target)
    41|        else:
    42|            shutil.copy2(item, target)
    43|
    44|
    45|def import_legacy_raw_history(raw_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    46|    imported_root = raw_root / 'imports' / 'legacy-workspace-raw-company-wiki'
    47|    result = {
    48|        'legacy_source': str(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT),
    49|        'import_root': str(imported_root),
    50|        'imported': False,
    51|        'copied_item_count': 0,
    52|        'cutover_map': str(raw_root / 'cutover-map.json'),
    53|        'legacy_exists': LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT.exists(),
    54|    }
    55|    if not LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT.exists():
    56|        return result
    57|
    58|    copy_tree_contents(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT, imported_root)
    59|    result['imported'] = True
    60|    result['copied_item_count'] = sum(1 for _ in imported_root.rglob('*'))
    61|    cutover_map = {
    62|        'cutover_at': now_iso(),
    63|        'policy': {
    64|            'historical_artifacts_are_immutable': True,
    65|            'old_root_paths_preserved_in_historical_artifacts': True,
    66|            'silent_path_rewrite_for_raw_evidence': False,
    67|            'canonical_provenance_exposed_via_cutover_mapping': True,
    68|        },
    69|        'legacy_source_root': str(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT),
    70|        'canonical_import_root': str(imported_root),
    71|    }
    72|    write_json(raw_root / 'cutover-map.json', cutover_map)
    73|    append_contract_log(
    74|        'company-wiki',
    75|        'raw-import',
    76|        root=raw_root.parent,
    77|        legacy_source=LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT,
    78|        import_root=imported_root,
    79|    )
    80|    return result
    81|
    82|
    83|def main() -> None:
    84|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Refresh the canonical Hermes-style company wiki from Slack, Trello, GitHub, Orbi, and Notion snapshots.')
    85|    parser.add_argument('--root', default=str(COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT))
    86|    parser.add_argument('--slack-team', default='')
    87|    parser.add_argument('--archive-mode', choices=['archive', 'incremental'], default='incremental')
    88|    parser.add_argument('--slack-history-limit', type=int, default=0, help='Per-channel conversations.history page size. Uses mode defaults when omitted.')
    89|    parser.add_argument('--slack-history-chunk-size', type=int, default=100, help='Persist conversations.history payloads in chunked raw item sizes.')
    90|    parser.add_argument('--slack-channel-limit', type=int, default=0, help='Max candidate channels to process this run. Uses mode defaults when omitted.')
    91|    parser.add_argument('--github-org', default='orbisoptimus')
    92|    parser.add_argument('--notion-force-catalog-refresh', action='store_true')
    93|    parser.add_argument('--notion-page-batch-size', type=int, default=25)
    94|    parser.add_argument('--notion-data-source-batch-size', type=int, default=10)
    95|    args = parser.parse_args()
    96|
    97|    root = Path(args.root)
    98|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root)
    99|    ensure_contract_ready('company-wiki', root=root)
   100|    paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
   101|    generated_at = now_iso()
   102|    TMP_ROOT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   103|
   104|    import_result = import_legacy_raw_history(paths['raw'])
   105|
   106|    run_manifest: dict[str, Any] = {
   107|        'generated_at': generated_at,
   108|        'canonical_root': str(paths['root']),
   109|        'sources': {},
   110|        'import': import_result,
   111|        'build_root': str(paths['root']),
   112|    }
   113|    all_entries: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   114|
   115|    try:
   116|        slack_result = slack_snapshot.capture(
   117|            raw_root=paths['raw'],
   118|            slack_team=args.slack_team,
   119|            archive_mode=args.archive_mode,
   120|            history_limit=(args.slack_history_limit or None),
   121|            chunk_size=max(1, args.slack_history_chunk_size),
   122|            channel_limit=(args.slack_channel_limit or None),
   123|        )
   124|        all_entries.extend(slack_result['entries'])
   125|        run_manifest['sources']['slack'] = {
   126|            'status': slack_result['status'],
   127|            'manifest': slack_result['manifest'],
   128|            'archive_mode': args.archive_mode,
   129|            'history_summary': slack_result.get('history_summary', {}),
   130|        }
   131|    except CompanyWikiError as exc:
   132|        run_manifest['sources']['slack'] = {'status': 'blocked', 'blocker': str(exc)}
   133|
   134|    try:
   135|        trello_result = trello_snapshot.capture(repo_root=ROOT, raw_root=paths['raw'])
   136|        all_entries.extend(trello_result['entries'])

{"path":"workspace/docs/plans/2026-04-14-company-wiki-skill-cron-redefinition-plan.md","content":"# Company Wiki / Skill / Cron 재정의 계획서

> 기준: canonical company-wiki를 단일 회사 기억면으로 두고, `slack-org-wiki`는 Slack source-adapter lane으로 내리며, cron은 Hermes-first refresh 운영선으로 재정의한다.

## 1. Situation

현재 구조는 이미 많이 정리돼 있다. 확인된 상태는 아래와 같다.

- canonical wiki root는 `workspace/company-wiki/`다.
- `workspace/company-wiki/SCHEMA.md`는 읽기 순서를 `queries → concepts → entities → comparisons`로 정의하고 있다.
- `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`는 executive-first landing 구조를 갖고 있고, 2026-04-14T04:40:04Z 기준 page count 100, queries 2, concepts 6, entities 91, comparisons 1이다.
- 최신 top-level run manifest `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/20260414T041702Z.json` 기준 source 상태는 Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion 모두 `ok`다.
- 다만 Slack은 workspace-wide admin inventory가 아니라 public-channel bot-accessible fallback 비중이 크다. 같은 manifest에서 `oversight.conversations.list`와 `admin.conversations.search`는 `missing_scope` / `not_allowed_token_type`로 막혀 있다.
- system crontab에는 현재 `17 * * * * ... run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`가 살아 있으며, hourly incremental refresh가 canonical refresh line으로 동작 중이다.
- `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`는 이미 Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi + Notion canonical substrate를 정의한다.
- 반면 `workspace/skills/create_slack_cron.md`는 아직 `zeroclaw agent -m`, `zeroclaw channel send`, `delivery.announce` 우회 논리 중심이라 현재 Hermes-first runtime 문맥과 어긋난다.
- `workspace/skills/slack-org-wiki.md`는 CLI-admin inventory를 강조하지만, 실제 운영 기본선은 bot-token 기반 canonical refresh다. skill framing을 더 분명히 source-adapter/diagnostic lane 쪽으로 고정할 필요가 있다.

## 2. 핵심 판단

### A. 위키 자체는 “재구축”이 아니라 “운영면 재정의” 단계다
루트와 빌드 파이프라인은 이미 canonical 형태에 들어왔다. 지금 필요한 건 새 위키를 만드는 일이 아니라, **무엇이 canonical이고 무엇이 helper인지 더 강하게 규정**하는 일이다.

### B. 가장 큰 부채는 skill/cron 설명면의 비대칭이다
실제 시스템은 Hermes-first canonical refresh로 움직이는데, 일부 skill 설명은 여전히 ZeroClaw-era delivery와 Slack CLI 중심 사고를 남기고 있다. 이건 운영자에게 잘못된 mental model을 준다.

### C. cron은 “Slack 전송 자동화”와 “canonical refresh 운영”을 분리해서 관리해야 한다
지금 live cron은 company-wiki refresh cron이다. 이건 Slack 보고용 cron이 아니라 **substrate maintenance cron**이다. 따라서 wiki 운영 cron과 Slack delivery cron을 한 계층에서 설명하면 안 된다.

---

## 3. 재정의 목표

### Goal 1. Wiki 계층 재정의
`company-wiki`를 다음 한 문장으로 고정한다.

> 회사 전체의 canonical memory surface. Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion raw를 한 substrate로 정규화하고, 경영 판단은 여기서 읽는다.

### Goal 2. Skill 계층 재정의
- `company-wiki` = top-level user-facing company memory / build-query surface
- `slack-org-wiki` = Slack inventory/bootstrap/history-access diagnosis를 위한 source-adapter lane
- `create_slack_cron` = Hermes cronjob 또는 Hermes-safe shell wrapper 기반 Slack delivery automation surface

### Goal 3. Cron 계층 재정의
cron을 두 종류로 나눈다.

1. **substrate cron**
   - canonical company-wiki refresh 유지 목적
   - 현재 `run_company_wiki_refresh.sh` + system crontab line이 여기에 해당
2. **delivery cron**
   - 사람에게 정기 보고/알림을 보내는 목적
   - `create_slack_cron`이 다루는 대상

이 둘을 분리해야 운영·문서·장애 대응이 단순해진다.

---

## 4. 구체 재정의안

## 4-1. Canonical wiki 재정의

### 유지할 것
- canonical root: `workspace/company-wiki/`
- authority docs: `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, `log.md`
- executive-first reading order
- manual `queries/` preservation
- source coverage: Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion

### 추가로 명확히 쓸 것
`workspace/company-wiki/SCHEMA.md`와 `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`에 아래 문장을 더 강하게 반영한다.

- `company-wiki`는 **read surface**이자 **refresh substrate**다.
- Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion은 모두 source adapter이며, 사용자에게는 source별 위키가 아니라 한 회사 위키로 노출된다.
- `slack-org-wiki`는 canonical root의 경쟁 위키가 아니라, Slack 쪽 ingestion/access diagnostics lane이다.
- cron은 company-wiki의 일부가 아니라, company-wiki freshness를 유지하는 운영장치다.

### 기대 효과
- “위키가 여러 개 있는 것처럼 보이는 문제” 제거
- 질문 라우팅이 `company-wiki` 중심으로 수렴
- 운영 readout과 source debugging이 분리됨

---

## 4-2. Skill 재정의

### 1) `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`

#### 바꿀 방향
문서 첫 30~50줄에서 아래를 더 직접적으로 선언한다.

- top-level company memory surface임
- source별 retrieval 차이를 숨기지 않되, 사용자-facing mental model은 하나의 canonical wiki임
- refresh cron, manual refresh, audit memo 작성, retained query 작성까지 이 skill의 운영 범위임

#### 추가할 섹션
- **Operating modes**
  - query mode
  - refresh mode
  - executive audit mode
  - source-gap diagnosis mode
- **Cron contract**
  - `run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`는 substrate cron entrypoint
  - Slack delivery cron과 혼동하지 말 것
- **Readiness rubric**
  - source ok/blocked
  - freshness
  - query coverage
  - executive memo coverage

#### 이유
현재도 강한 skill이지만, “이게 회사 전체 canonical 운영 표면”이라는 선언을 맨 앞에서 더 세게 못 박아야 한다.

### 2) `workspace/skills/slack-org-wiki.md`

#### 바꿀 방향
role 정의를 다음처럼 더 좁힌다.

> `slack-org-wiki`는 Slack live access/inventory/bootstrap/diagnostic lane이다. canonical company memory 자체는 `company-wiki`가 담당한다.

#### 추가할 것
- 언제 `company-wiki`로 라우팅하고, 언제 `slack-org-wiki`로 라우팅하는지 decision table
- inventory / join / history-access / admin-scope diagnosis 중심 예시
- “현재 verified scope는 public-channel bot-accessible history + metadata이고, admin inventory는 blocked일 수 있음”을 최신 manifest 기준으로 문서에 반영

#### 이유
현재 skill은 여전히 Slack-derived wiki build 쪽 인상을 준다. 이제는 그 역할보다 **source-adapter + capability diagnosis** 성격이 더 정확하다.

### 3) `workspace/skills/create_slack_cron.md`

#### 바꿀 방향
이 스킬은 사실상 전면 개편 대상이다.

현재 문제:
- ZeroClaw 명령어 중심
- `delivery.announce` 우회 논리 중심
- `zeroclaw channel send`를 전제
- timezone default가 UTC로 적혀 있어 현재 사용자 타임존/운영 문맥과도 덜 맞음

#### 새 역할 정의
> `create_slack_cron`은 Hermes runtime에서 Slack 전달이 필요한 정기 자동화를 설계/설치/검증하는 skill이다. 먼저 Hermes `cronjob`을 검토하고, 실제 Slack landing 보장이 더 중요할 때만 shell wrapper를 쓴다.

#### 새 표준 패턴
1. **기본안**: Hermes `cronjob` 사용
   - self-contained prompt
   - origin 또는 명시 채널 delivery
   - model/skills attached
2. **대체안**: shell wrapper
   - local file output
   - Slack external upload helper 또는 검증된 전송 루트 사용
3. **검증 규칙**
   - schedule 생성 확인
   - 실제 한 번 run 또는 dry-run evidence
   - Slack landing 성공 여부 분리 보고

#### 문서에 반드시 들어갈 것
- substrate cron과 delivery cron의 차이
- company-wiki refresh cron은 이 skill의 주 업무가 아니라 `company-wiki` 운영 contract에 속한다는 점
- shell wrapper를 쓸 때도 Hermes-first naming/path를 쓸 것

#### 이유
이 스킬이 그대로 있으면 운영 문맥이 Hermes가 아니라 ZeroClaw로 되돌아간다.

---

## 4-3. Cron 재정의

### A. 유지 대상: company-wiki substrate cron
현재 crontab line:

`17 * * * * COMPANY_WIKI_SLACK_TEAM=moveis COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE=incremental COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_PAGE_BATCH_SIZE=25 COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_BATCH_SIZE=10 /home/orbibot/.zeroclaw/workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh >> /home/orbibot/.zeroclaw/workspace/logs/company-wiki-refresh-cron.log 2>&1`

이 line은 유지하되 의미를 다음처럼 재정의한다.

- 목적: Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion canonical freshness 유지
- 성격: delivery cron이 아니라 substrate maintenance cron
- 성공 조건: run manifest 생성 + build ok + index/log 업데이트

### B. 개선 대상: company-wiki cron installer/wrapper
대상 파일:
- `workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`
- `workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh`

#### 개선 포인트
1. 스크립트 헤더/주석에 “canonical substrate refresh”임을 명시
2. 로그 경로/manifest 경로/최근 run 확인 명령을 문서화
3. install script가 현재 라인을 치환하는 동작은 이미 좋으므로 유지
4. 필요 시 `COMPANY_WIKI_GITHUB_ORG`, `COMPANY_WIKI_NOTION_FORCE_CATALOG_REFRESH` 같은 env 확장 여지를 문서화

### C. 새로 분리해서 문서화할 것: report/delivery cron
이는 crontab에 지금 없더라도 운영 문서상 별도 계층으로 정의해야 한다.

- 목적: Slack thread/channel로 요약 전달
- 도구: Hermes `cronjob` 우선
- 실패 조건: run 성공이 아니라 delivery 실패
- 소유 skill: `create_slack_cron`

---

## 5. 파일별 수정 계획

### Phase 1 — 선언 정리
1. `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`
   - role/front section 재작성
   - operating modes 추가
   - cron contract 추가
2. `workspace/skills/slack-org-wiki.md`
   - role narrowing
   - routing decision table 추가
   - latest verified Slack scope/caveat 반영
3. `workspace/skills/create_slack_cron.md`
   - Hermes-first로 전면 rewrite
   - `zeroclaw`/`delivery.announce` 중심 문구 제거

### Phase 2 — 운영 문서 정합성
4. `workspace/AGENTS.md`
   - quick capability wiring에서 `create_slack_cron` 설명을 Hermes-first delivery automation으로 갱신
   - Slack org/wiki invariants에 `company-wiki` vs `slack-org-wiki` 분리를 더 명확히 반영
5. `workspace/company-wiki/queries/`
   - 이번 재정의를 retained memo로 1개 추가
   - 이후 canonical index에서 바로 읽히게 함

### Phase 3 — cron contract 정리
6. `workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`
   - 주석/usage 문구 보강
7. `workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh`
   - substrate cron naming/description 보강
8. 필요 시 `workspace/bin/uninstall_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh`
   - 같은 naming 체계로 정합화

---

## 6. 실행 우선순위

### P0
- `create_slack_cron.md` Hermes-first 재작성
- `slack-org-wiki.md`의 source-adapter lane 정의 강화
- `company-wiki.md`에 canonical / cron contract 명시 강화

### P1
- AGENTS routing 문구 업데이트
- company-wiki retained memo 추가

### P2
- refresh cron wrapper/install script의 설명 정비
- cron health check 커맨드/운영 체크리스트 추가

---

## 7. 검증 기준

재정의 완료 판정은 아래로 한다.

### Wiki
- `company-wiki` 관련 질문이 하나의 canonical surface로 일관되게 설명된다.
- index/queries에 이번 재정의 memo가 살아 있다.

### Skills
- `company-wiki.md`에서 canonical ownership이 명확하다.
- `slack-org-wiki.md`가 competing wiki root처럼 읽히지 않는다.
- `create_slack_cron.md`에 ZeroClaw 중심 설명이 남아 있지 않다.

### Cron
- `crontab -l`로 substrate cron line이 유지된다.
- `run_company_wiki_refresh.sh` 설명이 substrate maintenance 목적과 일치한다.
- Slack delivery automation은 별도 category로 설명된다.

---

## 8. Recommendation

한 줄로 정리하면 이거다.

**위키는 하나(`company-wiki`), Slack은 어댑터(`slack-org-wiki`), 크론은 두 종류(substrate refresh / Slack delivery)로 분리**하면 된다.

가장 먼저 손봐야 할 건 `create_slack_cron.md`다. 현재 이 파일만 봐도 운영 mental model이 ZeroClaw 시절로 끌려간다. 그 다음이 `slack-org-wiki.md` 역할 축소/정밀화, 마지막이 `company-wiki.md`와 AGENTS의 선언 강화다.

---

## 9. 근거 파일 / 증거

- `workspace/company-wiki/SCHEMA.md`
- `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`
- `workspace/company-wiki/queries/2026-04-13-initial-wiki-reordering.md`
- `workspace/company-wiki/queries/2026-04-14-notion-canonical-integration.md`
- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/20260414T041702Z.json`
- `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`
- `workspace/skills/slack-org-wiki.md`
- `workspace/skills/create_slack_cron.md`
- `workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`
- `workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh`
- live: `crontab -l` (현재 hourly company-wiki refresh line 확인)
"}

metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [planning, design, implementation, workflow, documentation]
    related_skills: [subagent-driven-development, test-driven-development, requesting-code-review]
---

# Writing Implementation Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the implementer has zero context for the codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need: which files to touch, complete code, testing commands, docs to check, how to verify. Give them bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume the implementer is a skilled developer but knows almost nothing about the toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Core principle:** A good plan makes implementation obvious. If someone has to guess, the plan is incomplete.

## When to Use

**Always use before:**
- Implementing multi-step features
- Breaking down complex requirements
- Delegating to subagents via subagent-driven-development

**Don't skip when:**
- Feature seems simple (assumptions cause bugs)
- You plan to implement it yourself (future you needs guidance)
- Working alone (documentation matters)

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each task = 2-5 minutes of focused work.**

Every step is one action:
- "Write the failing test" — step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" — step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" — step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" — step
- "Commit" — step

**Too big:**
```markdown
### Task 1: Build authentication system
[50 lines of code across 5 files]
```

**Right size:**
```markdown
### Task 1: Create User model with email field
[10 lines, 1 file]

### Task 2: Add password hash field to User
[8 lines, 1 file]

### Task 3: Create password hashing utility
[15 lines, 1 file]
```

## Plan Document Structure

### Header (Required)

Every plan MUST start with:

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For Hermes:** Use subagent-driven-development skill to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

### Task Structure

Each task follows this format:

````markdown
### Task N: [Descriptive Name]

**Objective:** What this task accomplishes (one sentence)

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/new_file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:45-67` (line numbers if known)
- Test: `tests/path/to/test_file.py`

**Step 1: Write failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

**Step 2: Run test to verify failure**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_specific_behavior -v`
Expected: FAIL — "function not defined"

**Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

**Step 4: Run test to verify pass**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_specific_behavior -v`
Expected: PASS

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Writing Process

### Step 1: Understand Requirements

Read and understand:
- Feature requirements
- Design documents or user description
- Acceptance criteria
- Constraints

### Step 2: Explore the Codebase

Use Hermes tools to understand the project:

```python
# Understand project structure
search_files("*.py", target="files", path="src/")

# Look at similar features
search_files("similar_pattern", path="src/", file_glob="*.py")

# Check existing tests
search_files("*.py", target="files", path="tests/")

# Read key files
read_file("src/app.py")
```

### Step 3: Design Approach

Decide:
- Architecture pattern
- File organization
- Dependencies needed
- Testing strategy

### Step 4: Write Tasks

Create tasks in order:
1. Setup/infrastructure
2. Core functionality (TDD for each)
3. Edge cases
4. Integration
5. Cleanup/documentation

### Step 5: Add Complete Details

For each task, include:
- **Exact file paths** (not "the config file" but `src/config/settings.py`)
- **Complete code examples** (not "add validation" but the actual code)
- **Exact commands** with expected output
- **Verification steps** that prove the task works

### Step 6: Review the Plan

Check:
- [ ] Tasks are sequential and logical
- [ ] Each task is bite-sized (2-5 min)
- [ ] File paths are exact
- [ ] Code examples are complete (copy-pasteable)
- [ ] Commands are exact with expected output
- [ ] No missing context
- [ ] DRY, YAGNI, TDD principles applied

### Step 7: Save the Plan

```bash
mkdir -p docs/plans
# Save plan to docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-feature-name.md
git add docs/plans/
git commit -m "docs: add implementation plan for [feature]"
```

## Principles

### DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)

**Bad:** Copy-paste validation in 3 places
**Good:** Extract validation function, use everywhere

### YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It)

**Bad:** Add "flexibility" for future requirements
**Good:** Implement only what's needed now

```python
# Bad — YAGNI violation
class User:
    def __init__(self, name, email):
        self.name = name
        self.email = email
        self.preferences = {}  # Not needed yet!
        self.metadata = {}     # Not needed yet!

# Good — YAGNI
class User:
    def __init__(self, name, email):
        self.name = name
        self.email = email
```

### TDD (Test-Driven Development)

Every task that produces code should include the full TDD cycle:
1. Write failing test
2. Run to verify failure
3. Write minimal code
4. Run to verify pass

See `test-driven-development` skill for details.

### Frequent Commits

Commit after every task:
```bash
git add [files]
git commit -m "type: description"
```

## Common Mistakes

### Vague Tasks

**Bad:** "Add authentication"
**Good:** "Create User model with email and password_hash fields"

### Incomplete Code

**Bad:** "Step 1: Add validation function"
**Good:** "Step 1: Add validation function" followed by the complete function code

### Missing Verification

**Bad:** "Step 3: Test it works"
**Good:** "Step 3: Run `pytest tests/test_auth.py -v`, expected: 3 passed"

### Missing File Paths

**Bad:** "Create the model file"
**Good:** "Create: `src/models/user.py`"

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer the execution approach:

**"Plan complete and saved. Ready to execute using subagent-driven-development — I'll dispatch a fresh subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance then code quality). Shall I proceed?"**

When executing, use the `subagent-driven-development` skill:
- Fresh `delegate_task` per task with full context
- Spec compliance review after each task
- Code quality review after spec passes
- Proceed only when both reviews approve

## Remember

```
Bite-sized tasks (2-5 min each)
Exact file paths
Complete code (copy-pasteable)
Exact commands with expected output
Verification steps
DRY, YAGNI, TDD
Frequent commits
```

**A good plan makes implementation obvious.**

# AGENTS.md — Orbiracle CEO Staff / Strategy Office

## Every Session (required)

Before doing anything else:

1. Read `SOUL.md` — this is who you are
2. Read `USER.md` — this is who you're helping
3. Use `memory_recall` for recent context (daily notes are on-demand)
4. If in MAIN SESSION (direct chat): `MEMORY.md` is already injected

Don't ask permission. Just do it.

## Core Operating Rules

- Do the work first. Deliver results, not intentions.
- Ban these patterns in user-facing replies unless the user explicitly asks for hypotheticals: **"다음부터는"**, **"앞으로는"**, **"원한다면"**, **"if you want"**, **"I can"**, **"let me know"**.
- When the user asks for a change to workflow, docs, skills, or files, update the relevant file(s) immediately and report the concrete result.
- Prefer acting, retrieving, editing, checking, and returning outputs over describing what could be done.
- Completion means delivery in the correct place/channel/thread, not merely saying a file was created.
- Write down anything worth remembering; mental notes do not survive restarts.
- The workspace default working directory is already `~/.zeroclaw/workspace`; do not prepend redundant `cd /home/orbibot/.zeroclaw/workspace` before local commands unless changing directories is actually necessary.

## Default Operating Stance

- Default persona: **Orbi / 문영사 / 무브의 CEO Staff 겸 전략기획실장**.
- Start from the management question first: what decision needs to be made, what tradeoff matters, and what action follows.
- Treat Orbi as one important source, not the automatic center of every answer.
- Prefer cross-source judgment across Slack, Trello, GitHub, company wiki/docs, Orbi, and the broader web when that improves the answer.
- When the user asks about company direction, priorities, risk, execution, coordination, or performance, answer like staff-to-CEO briefing, not like a community crawler.

## Cross-Source Accuracy Mandate

- For Slack, Trello, GitHub, and similar workplace/tooling questions, the primary goal is **accurate answers grounded in live evidence**.
- Use every relevant available source that can improve correctness: local wrappers, official CLIs, API-backed helpers, workspace docs, retrieved artifacts, and cross-checking between systems.
- Do not answer from vague memory when live retrieval is possible and the question depends on current state.
- Prefer source fusion over single-source guesses: compare Slack chatter, Trello board state, GitHub activity, and local notes when the question spans multiple systems.
- Distinguish clearly between verified facts, inference, and missing evidence.
- Stay read-only unless the user explicitly asks for an external action or mutation.

## Memory System

You wake up fresh each session. These files ARE your continuity:

- **Daily notes:** `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` — raw logs (accessed via memory tools)
- **Long-term:** `MEMORY.md` — curated memories (auto-injected in main session)

Capture what matters. Decisions, context, things to remember.
Skip secrets unless asked to keep them.

### Write It Down — No Mental Notes!

- When someone says "remember this" -> update daily file or `MEMORY.md`
- When you learn a lesson -> update `AGENTS.md`, `TOOLS.md`, or the relevant skill

## Safety & Action Boundary

- Don't exfiltrate private data. Ever.
- Don't run destructive commands without asking.
- `trash` > `rm` (recoverable beats gone forever)
- Safe to do freely: read files, explore, organize, learn, search the web.
- Ask first for actions that leave the machine, such as sending emails, tweets, or posts.
- When in doubt, ask.

## Group Chats

- Participate, don't dominate.
- Stay silent for casual banter or when someone already answered.
- In Slack and other group-chat contexts, default to replying only when explicitly tagged/mentioned unless the user has clearly asked for proactive participation in that thread.

## Output Length & Delivery

- Keep every chat message under **3000 characters**.
- If a response would exceed that limit, split it into multiple sub-3000-character messages or prefer a file-based deliverable.
- For long analyses, write the full content to a file first, then send the file as an attachment instead of pasting a long wall of text.
- Prefer concise executive summaries in chat, with the detailed analysis living in the attached file.
- Plain Slack text replies use the normal message path; do not fake attachments by dumping a giant blob into chat.

### Slack file delivery

- Use Slack's external upload flow, not deprecated `files.upload`:
  1. `files.getUploadURLExternal`
  2. upload bytes to the returned URL
  3. `files.completeUploadExternal`
- Preferred local helper: `/Users/yeachanheo/.zeroclaw/scripts/slack_file_upload.swift --file <path> --channel <id> [--comment ...] [--thread-ts ...]`
- If Slack upload fails (for example `missing_scope` or outage), fall back to a short chat summary plus the on-disk file path and say upload failed.

## Skills

Use `read_skill` when available, or `file_read` on a skill file, for full details.
Keep local notes (SSH hosts, device names, environment specifics) in `TOOLS.md`.

### Quick capability wiring

- CEO-staff / strategy cross-source brief across company surfaces → `skills/company-wiki.md`, `skills/github-org-intel.md`, `skills/trello-portfolio-observer.md`
- Company-wide canonical wiki build/query across Slack + Trello + GitHub → `skills/company-wiki.md`
- Slack live org/workspace reading, admin inventory, or source ingestion adapter → `skills/slack-org-wiki.md`
- Slack file/thread upload delivery → `skills/slack-file-send.md`
- Slack scheduled delivery/cron wiring → `skills/create_slack_cron.md`
- Trello live portfolio/board health scan → `skills/trello-portfolio-observer.md`
- Trello wiki-style executive readout from layered Trello evidence → `skills/trello-wiki-exec-report.md`
- GitHub org/repo/contributor portfolio analysis → `skills/github-org-intel.md`

When a user asks whether Slack, Trello, GitHub, or similar internal surfaces are accessible, answer that these wired skill surfaces exist and route to them explicitly instead of describing only generic analysis ability.

### Top-level company/management surfaces

Use these first when the user is asking a company, management, or strategy question:

1. `skills/company-wiki.md` — cross-source company memory / wiki build-query surface across Slack + Trello + GitHub
2. `skills/github-org-intel.md` — org/repo/contributor execution surface grounded in GitHub evidence
3. `skills/trello-portfolio-observer.md` — board/portfolio health, dependency, and execution-readout surface
4. `skills/trello-wiki-exec-report.md` — company-wiki-grounded Trello executive readout

### Orbi specialist surfaces

Use these when the question is specifically about Orbi user signal, community risk, student demand, or Orbi-grounded content/business analysis:

1. `skills/orbi-chief-of-staff.md` — company priorities, executive briefings, directive queues, cross-functional work
2. `skills/orbi-risk-triage.md` — safety, legal, reputation, and moderation-adjacent risk monitoring

### Internal Orbi specialist lanes

- `skills/orbi-risk-scan.md` — candidate collection only
- `skills/orbi-risk-assess.md` — body/context assessment only
- `skills/orbi-risk-brief.md` — short operator/executive brief only

### GitHub surface

- `skills/github-org-intel.md` — internal GitHub org / repo / contributor / portfolio analysis grounded in read-only `gh` CLI evidence

### Slack org/wiki surface

Use `skills/company-wiki.md` as the canonical company wiki surface for cross-source build/query over Slack + Trello + GitHub.
Use `skills/slack-org-wiki.md` only when the request is specifically about live Slack inventory, auth/bootstrap state, public-channel join, or Slack-side ingestion mechanics.
Treat `slack-public-wiki`, `slack-llm-wiki-retrieval`, and `slack-channel-join` as legacy/specialized helper surfaces, not first-choice user-facing routes.

### Webnovel surface

- `skills/orbi-webnovel-

{"content": "     1|# Skill — create_slack_cron
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`create_slack_cron` creates **reliable Slack-delivery cron automations**.
     6|
     7|> **Status note:** This skill still contains some legacy ZeroClaw-era examples.
     8|> For new work in this repo, treat the preferred model as **Hermes-first**:
     9|> use Hermes `cronjob` when direct scheduled delivery is sufficient, and use a
    10|> shell wrapper only when you need a separately verifiable local-output → Slack
    11|> send path.
    12|>
    13|> Also distinguish two cron classes:
    14|> 1. **substrate cron** — keeps canonical systems like `company-wiki` fresh
    15|> 2. **delivery cron** — sends human-facing reports/alerts into Slack
    16|>
    17|> This skill is for **delivery cron**, not for the canonical `company-wiki`
    18|> refresh cron.
    19|
    20|Use this when the output must actually land in Slack on schedule and the safer
    21|path is:
    22|
    23|1. shell cron starts
    24|2. local script runs `zeroclaw agent -m "<prompt>"`
    25|3. output is saved to disk
    26|4. `zeroclaw channel send` posts it to Slack
    27|
    28|## When to Use
    29|
    30|Use for prompts like:
    31|
    32|- “슬랙으로 가는 크론 만들어줘”
    33|- “매일/매주 보고를 Slack에 자동 발송해줘”
    34|- “cron delivery 말고 직접 Slack 보내는 방식으로 붙여줘”
    35|- “문제 있는 announce cron 말고 우회 경로로 자동화해줘”
    36|
    37|## Standard Build Pattern
    38|
    39|For each Slack cron, create:
    40|
    41|1. `workspace/prompts/<name>.prompt.txt`
    42|2. `workspace/bin/send_<name>.sh`
    43|3. a shell cron job that calls that script
    44|
    45|## Script Contract
    46|
    47|The shell script should usually:
    48|
    49|1. set `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`
    50|2. read a prompt file
    51|3. run:
    52|   - `cd "$ROOT" && RUST_LOG=error zeroclaw agent -m "$prompt"`
    53|4. save stdout to:
    54|   - `workspace/reports/` for report-style outputs
    55|   - `workspace/alerts/` for alert-style outputs
    56|5. save stderr to `workspace/logs/`
    57|6. send stdout with:
    58|   - `zeroclaw channel send "$text" --channel-id slack --recipient <CHANNEL_ID>`
    59|
    60|## Naming Convention
    61|
    62|- Prompt file: `workspace/prompts/<slug>.prompt.txt`
    63|- Script file: `workspace/bin/send_<slug>.sh`
    64|- Report file: `workspace/reports/<slug>-<date>.md`
    65|- Alert file: `workspace/alerts/<slug>-<timestamp>.md`
    66|
    67|## Cron Convention
    68|
    69|- Prefer **shell cron** over agent cron for Slack delivery.
    70|- If an older agent cron already exists for the same purpose, pause it.
    71|- Default timezone to `UTC` unless the user requests another timezone.
    72|- After creation, test the script once manually if safe.
    73|
    74|## Output Checklist
    75|
    76|When finishing, report:
    77|
    78|1. created prompt path
    79|2. created script path
    80|3. cron job id
    81|4. schedule + timezone
    82|5. whether a live send test succeeded
    83|
    84|## Guardrails
    85|
    86|- Do not claim Slack delivery is working unless `zeroclaw channel send` or the
    87|  wrapper script was actually tested successfully.
    88|- Keep prompt text outside the shell script when possible.
    89|- Prefer concise, operator-usable Slack outputs.

- “매일/매주 보고를 Slack에 자동 발송해줘”
- “cron delivery 말고 직접 Slack 보내는 방식으로 붙여줘”
- “문제 있는 announce cron 말고 우회 경로로 자동화해줘”

## Standard Build Pattern

For each Slack cron, create:

1. `workspace/prompts/<name>.prompt.txt`
2. `workspace/bin/send_<name>.sh`
3. a shell cron job that calls that script

## Script Contract

The shell script should usually:

1. set `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`
2. read a prompt file
3. run:
   - `cd "$ROOT" && RUST_LOG=error zeroclaw agent -m "$prompt"`
4. save stdout to:
   - `workspace/reports/` for report-style outputs
   - `workspace/alerts/` for alert-style outputs
5. save stderr to `workspace/logs/`
6. send stdout with:
   - `zeroclaw channel send "$text" --channel-id slack --recipient <CHANNEL_ID>`

## Naming Convention

- Prompt file: `workspace/prompts/<slug>.prompt.txt`
- Script file: `workspace/bin/send_<slug>.sh`
- Report file: `workspace/reports/<slug>-<date>.md`
- Alert file: `workspace/alerts/<slug>-<timestamp>.md`

## Cron Convention

- Prefer **shell cron** over agent cron for Slack delivery.
- If an older agent cron already exists for the same purpose, pause it.
- Default timezone to `UTC` unless the user requests another timezone.
- After creation, test the script once manually if safe.

## Output Checklist

When finishing, report:

1. created prompt path
2. created script path
3. cron job id
4. schedule + timezone
5. whether a live send test succeeded

## Guardrails

- Do not claim Slack delivery is working unless `zeroclaw channel send` or the
  wrapper script was actually tested successfully.
- Keep prompt text outside the shell script when possible.
- Prefer concise, operator-usable Slack outputs.
","new_string":"# Skill — create_slack_cron

## Role

`create_slack_cron` creates **reliable Slack-delivery automations in a Hermes-first runtime**.

This skill is for **delivery cron** only.
It is **not** the owner of the canonical `company-wiki` refresh loop.

Always separate two cron classes:

1. **substrate cron** — keeps canonical systems fresh
   - example: `workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`
2. **delivery cron** — sends human-facing reports, alerts, or summaries into Slack

If the task is “keep the wiki fresh,” route to `company-wiki`.
If the task is “send a scheduled report into Slack,” route here.

## Preferred execution order

### 1. Default path: Hermes `cronjob`

Use Hermes `cronjob` first when:
- the prompt can be made self-contained
- delivery can target the current chat or an explicit Slack destination
- local intermediate files are optional rather than mandatory

Standard pattern:
1. write a self-contained prompt
2. attach relevant skills if needed
3. create the schedule with `cronjob`
4. run once manually when verification matters
5. confirm the job id, schedule, destination, and test result

### 2. Fallback path: shell wrapper

Use a shell wrapper only when one of these is true:
- the user explicitly wants a file-backed local audit trail
- Slack landing must go through a separately testable upload/send helper
- the automation depends on local scripts or environment loading outside normal cronjob execution

In that case, create:
1. `workspace/prompts/<slug>.prompt.txt`
2. `workspace/bin/send_<slug>.sh`
3. a system crontab entry that calls that script

## When to Use

Use for prompts like:
- “슬랙으로 가는 크론 만들어줘”
- “매일/매주 보고를 Slack에 자동 발송해줘”
- “이 보고를 스레드로 정기 전달해줘”
- “delivery 성공 여부까지 검증해서 붙여줘”

## Hermes cronjob contract

Preferred fields to decide and report:
- job name
- self-contained prompt
- schedule
- destination (`origin`, explicit Slack target, or local-only)
- attached skills
- one-time validation run result when applicable

Guidance:
- prefer the current conversation as the destination unless the user specifies another channel/thread
- keep prompts self-contained because cron runs do not inherit current chat context
- prefer the user’s operating timezone context when a timezone-sensitive schedule matters; do not silently default to UTC in user-facing planning

## Shell wrapper contract

If a wrapper is required, the script should usually:

1. set `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`
2. load env if needed
3. read a prompt file
4. run the Hermes-compatible execution path for the task
5. save stdout to a durable local path such as:
   - `workspace/reports/` for report-style outputs
   - `workspace/alerts/` for alert-style outputs
6. save stderr to `workspace/logs/`
7. send the result to Slack using a verified delivery helper

Preferred Slack delivery helper in this workspace:
- `/Users/yeachanheo/.zeroclaw/scripts/slack_file_upload.swift`

Do not hard-code legacy `zeroclaw channel send` as the default story.
If a legacy path is used for diagnosis or compatibility, label it explicitly as legacy.

## Naming Convention

- Prompt file: `workspace/prompts/<slug>.prompt.txt`
- Script file: `workspace/bin/send_<slug>.sh`
- Report file: `workspace/reports/<slug>-<date>.md`
- Alert file: `workspace/alerts/<slug>-<timestamp>.md`
- Log file: `workspace/logs/<slug>.log`

## Verification standard

Minimum acceptable verification:
1. confirm the job or crontab line exists
2. run once manually when safe, or trigger one immediate cronjob run
3. confirm whether Slack landing succeeded
4. separate **generation success** from **delivery success** in the report

Never collapse these into one generic “working” statement.

## Output Checklist

When finishing, report:
1. chosen pattern: `cronjob` or `shell wrapper`
2. created prompt path, if any
3. created script path, if any
4. cron job id or installed crontab line
5. schedule and destination
6. attached skills or execution dependencies
7. whether a verification run succeeded
8. whether Slack landing succeeded

## Guardrails

- Do not describe substrate refresh cron as a Slack delivery cron.
- Do not claim Slack delivery works unless an actual run reached Slack or the exact blocker is known.
- Keep prompt text outside the shell script when possible.
- Prefer concise operator-usable Slack outputs.
- Prefer Hermes-native scheduling before adding another shell cron.
"}

{"mode":"replace","new_string":"### Cron/manual wrapper

```bash
workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh
```

Operational reality in this workspace:
- The canonical wiki root is `workspace/company-wiki/`, but the current wrapper scripts are still anchored to `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`.
- Treat this as a migration-era layout: Hermes-style wiki structure on top of a ZeroClaw-rooted runtime path.
- When auditing readiness, check both Hermes cron state and system crontab state; an empty Hermes cron list does not mean the refresh is unscheduled.

## Cron contract

`company-wiki` owns the **substrate refresh contract**.
That means:
- `workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh` is the canonical substrate refresh entrypoint
- `workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` and `workspace/bin/uninstall_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` manage the system-crontab installation for that substrate loop
- this refresh cron is distinct from Slack delivery cron and should not be documented as a human-facing report sender

Cron invariant:
- `workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` installs the refresh into system crontab in `incremental` mode by default, currently at minute 17 of every hour unless overridden by `COMPANY_WIKI_CRON_SCHEDULE`.
- Use `COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE=archive` only for an intentional one-off/manual baseline catch-up, not the hourly cron loop.
- Invoke the installer as a shell script (`bash workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` or direct execute). Do not run `.sh` installers with `python3`.

## Canonical readiness rubric

Check all four dimensions:
1. **source status** — which adapters are `ok` vs `blocked`
2. **freshness** — latest run-manifest/build/index/log timestamps
3. **coverage** — whether queries/concepts/entities reflect the intended source mix
4. **executive usefulness** — whether retained memos exist for the management question at hand

Optional cron installers:

```bash
workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh
workspace/bin/uninstall_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh
```

---

# 2026-04-14 company wiki / skill / cron redefinition

## Situation
canonical `company-wiki` 자체는 이미 Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion을 한 루트로 묶는 방향으로 정리돼 있다. 문제는 설명면이 완전히 따라오지 못한 점이었다. 특히 `slack-org-wiki`가 source-adapter보다 더 큰 위키처럼 읽힐 여지가 있었고, `create_slack_cron`은 아직도 ZeroClaw-era delivery mental model을 강하게 남기고 있었다.

## Evidence snapshot
- `SCHEMA.md`와 `index.md`는 이미 canonical root + executive-first reading order를 반영한다.
- latest run manifest `20260414T041702Z.json` 기준 Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion은 모두 source entry를 갖고 있다.
- 같은 manifest에서 Slack admin/oversight inventory는 `missing_scope` / `not_allowed_token_type`로 막혀 있다. 즉 verified baseline은 bot-accessible public-channel inventory/history 일부다.
- system crontab에는 `17 * * * * ... run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`가 살아 있다. 이건 사람에게 보내는 delivery cron이 아니라 canonical substrate refresh cron이다.

## Analysis
### 1. 위키는 하나로 못 박아야 한다
사용자-facing company memory는 `company-wiki` 하나여야 한다. source별 스킬은 retrieval/diagnostics lane이지, 별도 위키 루트가 아니다.

### 2. Slack 스킬은 retrieval lane으로 좁혀야 한다
`slack-org-wiki`의 정확한 역할은 auth/bootstrap, inventory, join/history diagnosis, ingestion support다. retained memo나 canonical read surface는 `company-wiki`가 맡는 게 맞다.

### 3. cron도 둘로 나눠야 한다
- substrate cron: canonical freshness 유지
- delivery cron: 사람에게 Slack 보고/알림 전달

이 분리가 없으면 운영자가 refresh cron을 delivery surface로 오해하게 된다.

## Recommendation
- `company-wiki`를 top-level canonical memory surface로 선언 강화
- `slack-org-wiki`를 Slack source-adapter/diagnostic lane으로 재정의
- `create_slack_cron`을 Hermes-first delivery cron skill로 전면 재정의
- `AGENTS.md`에서도 delivery cron vs substrate cron을 분리 명시

## Result applied in this cycle
- `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`에 canonical ownership, operating modes, cron contract, readiness rubric를 강화했다.
- `workspace/skills/slack-org-wiki.md`를 source-adapter/diagnostic lane 중심으로 재정의했고 routing decision table과 verified operating reality를 추가했다.
- `workspace/skills/create_slack_cron.md`를 Hermes-first delivery cron skill로 재작성했다.
- `workspace/AGENTS.md`에서 `create_slack_cron` 설명을 delivery-cron surface로 수정하고, Slack org/wiki invariants에 substrate-vs-delivery 구분을 추가했다.
"}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — company-wiki
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`company-wiki` is the **top-level canonical company memory surface**.
     6|
     7|It owns:
     8|- one canonical Hermes-style root under `workspace/company-wiki/`
     9|- Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi + Notion source ingestion into one shared substrate
    10|- deduped raw/manifests under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    11|- derived wiki generation under sectioned directories (`entities/`, `concepts/`, `comparisons/`, `queries`)
    12|- one unified local query surface
    13|- authoritative `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, `log.md` at the canonical root
    14|- executive-first index ordering plus preservation of manually written `queries/` and `comparisons/` pages across rebuilds
    15|- the operating contract for refresh, audit, retained memo creation, and canonical readiness checks
    16|
    17|Use this when the user wants:
    18|- one company wiki/search surface instead of Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi silos
    19|- raw snapshot caching plus AI-generated wiki refresh
    20|- a canonical refresh path with honest source coverage and blockers
    21|- source-specific retrieval methods documented as one operating workflow
    22|- management-facing retained memos written back into the canonical root
    23|
    24|## Retrieval Methodology
    25|
    26|The company wiki is a **4-stage pipeline**:
    27|
    28|1. **Retrieve** — pull whatever each source can actually expose right now
    29|2. **Persist raw** — save retrievable source payloads under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    30|3. **Normalize** — convert source-specific payloads into one shared derived-entry schema
    31|4. **Build/query** — generate/search derived wiki pages under `workspace/company-wiki/`
    32|
    33|Default retrieval/search always targets the **derived wiki**, not raw payloads.
    34|
    35|## Canonical root contract
    36|
    37|`company-wiki` is governed by the canonical root itself:
    38|- `workspace/company-wiki/SCHEMA.md`
    39|- `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`
    40|- `workspace/company-wiki/log.md`
    41|
    42|Reference-only Hermes material may remain under `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/`, but it is not authoritative for active company-wiki flow.
    43|
    44|## Source Adapters
    45|
    46|Every external surface below is a **source adapter feeding one canonical wiki**, not a separate user-facing wiki root.
    47|
    48|### Slack adapter
    49|
    50|Current retrieval tiers:
    51|
    52|1. **Workspace public-channel metadata**
    53|   - Uses `conversations.list`
    54|   - Captures public channel inventory, membership flags, and channel `updated` timestamps
    55|   - This is the baseline guaranteed path in this workspace
    56|
    57|2. **Public-channel message history raw**
    58|   - Uses `conversations.history` via the configured bot-token path after public-channel join
    59|   - Current refresh path attempts joins on selected public channels and persists raw history snapshots for channels where access succeeds
    60|   - Initial archive baseline runs use `--archive-mode archive` and persist history in chunked raw records plus per-channel checkpoints for resume
    61|   - Archive mode now also probes broader Slack inventory surfaces (`oversight.conversations.list`, `admin.conversations.search`) **if available** and widens candidate channels when those scopes actually work
    62|   - Hourly cron stays `incremental` only and uses per-channel latest message watermarks rather than replaying baseline pages
    63|   - This is the preferred path when the goal is a real content-backed company wiki refresh rather than metadata-only bootstrap
    64|   - Coverage is still partial and capability-dependent per channel/scope, so manifests must record successes and blockers explicitly
    65|
    66|3. **Private/admin inventory**
    67|   - Only allowed when the exact API path/scopes are verified
    68|   - Must never be implied from CLI login alone
    69|
    70|Excluded:
    71|- DM / 1:1
    72|- any unverified private-channel message visibility
    73|
    74|### GitHub adapter
    75|
    76|Current retrieval order:
    77|
    78|1. `gh auth status` — auth/scope sanity check
    79|2. `gh repo list <org>` — visible repository inventory and recent push activity
    80|3. normalized repo summaries — top active execution surfaces promoted into canonical concepts/entities pages
    81|
    82|GitHub remains strictly **read-only** and is currently sourced through the local `gh` CLI path.
    83|
    84|### Orbi adapter
    85|
    86|Current retrieval order:
    87|
    88|1. public trending endpoint — `/api/v1/board/search/realtime`
    89|2. public hot-posts list — `/list/hot?page=1`
    90|3. normalized Orbi summaries — signal overview + trending-keyword pages + hot-post pages in the canonical wiki
    91|
    92|Orbi remains strictly **public-web read-only** in this workflow. It is a live external demand/sentiment surface, not an internal operating system.
    93|
    94|### Notion adapter
    95|
    96|Current retrieval order:
    97|
    98|1. `search` — accessible workspace inventory across pages and data sources
    99|2. resumable page-content export — batched recursive `blocks/{id}/children` capture for accessible pages
   100|3. resumable data-source export — batched `data_sources/{id}/query` row snapshots for accessible data sources
   101|4. normalized Notion summaries — workspace overview plus durable data-source/root-page pages in the canonical wiki
   102|
   103|Notion is treated as **read-only integration-backed workspace memory**. Raw exports must stay under `raw/notion/`; derived pages remain summary-first.
   104|Use the resumable checkpoint at `workspace/company-wiki/raw/notion/checkpoint.json` for long-running background backfills and cron-safe incremental progress.
   105|
   106|### Trello adapter
   107|
   108|Current retrieval order:
   109|
   110|1. `members/me` — auth/identity sanity check
   111|2. `members/me/organizations` — visible workspace/org list
   112|3. `members/me/boards` — visible board inventory
   113|4. `boards/<id>` — board detail
   114|5. `boards/<id>/members` — board member coverage
   115|6. `boards/<id>/cards` — board card snapshot
   116|
   117|Trello remains strictly **read-only**.
   118|
   119|## Raw Snapshot Contract
   120|
   121|Raw payloads live under:
   122|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/`
   123|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/trello/`
   124|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/github/`
   125|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/orbi/`
   126|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/notion/`
   127|
   128|Each source keeps:
   129|- `items/` — deduped raw payload records
   130|- `dedupe-index.json` — payload fingerprint index
   131|- `runs/manifest-*.json` — per-run source manifest
   132|- `channel-checkpoints/*.json` — per-channel archive/incremental resume state for Slack history capture
   133|
   134|Top-level run manifests live under:
   135|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/`
   136|
   137|Rules:
   138|- raw is source-labeled
   139|- raw is snapshot-based, not event-sourced
   140|- repeated identical payloads should dedupe rather than multiply
   141|- Slack history payloads are persisted as chunked `channel_history_chunk` raw items rather than one large per-channel blob
   142|- raw may be incomplete if source capability is incomplete; blockers must be recorded explicitly
   143|
   144|## Normalize Contract
   145|
   146|Normalized entries are derived from raw snapshots and must preserve:
   147|- `source_type`
   148|- `access_scope`
   149|- `source_refs`
   150|- `raw_manifest_ref`
   151|- factual summary fields (`facts`, `decisions`, `actions`, `open_questions`, etc.)
   152|
   153|What normalization does:
   154|- maps source-specific payloads into one shared wiki-entry shape
   155|- preserves provenance and caveats
   156|- avoids leaking raw bodies into derived page content
   157|
   158|## Operating modes
   159|
   160|### 1. Query mode
   161|- search the canonical derived pages
   162|- answer from the company-wiki surface first
   163|- expand to source-specific live retrieval only when freshness or gaps require it
   164|
   165|### 2. Refresh mode
   166|- run the canonical refresh pipeline
   167|- inspect source statuses, blockers, manifests, and build output
   168|- confirm freshness in `index.md`, `log.md`, and run manifests
   169|
   170|### 3. Executive audit mode
   171|- judge whether the wiki is decision-ready, not merely populated
   172|- write retained memos under `workspace/company-wiki/queries/` when the result is substantive
   173|- reorder attention toward queries/concepts before entities
   174|
   175|### 4. Source-gap diagnosis mode
   176|- identify whether missing coverage is caused by auth, scope, join state, adapter logic, or source-side limits
   177|- distinguish blocked source coverage from canonical-root defects
   178|
   179|## Query Semantics
   180|
   181|Default query path:
   182|
   183|```bash
   184|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   185|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   186|  --query "product risk"
   187|```
   188|
   189|Query semantics:
   190|- searches Hermes-style derived sections:
   191|  - `entities/`
   192|  - `concepts/`
   193|  - `comparisons/`
   194|  - `queries/`
   195|- does not search raw payload bodies by default
   196|- returns mixed-source results in one surface
   197|- source provenance is preserved inside the page content/refs
   198|
   199|Interpretation rules:
   200|- a Slack page reflects what was retrievable from Slack at refresh time
   201|- a Trello page reflects current-state Trello snapshots at refresh time
   202|- mixed-source hits should be read as one company-memory surface, not as identical-confidence evidence
   203|
   204|## Executive realignment / audit mode
   205|
   206|Use this mode when the user asks to "reorganize the wiki", "re-rank the wiki", "pull urgent action items", or otherwise wants a management-facing readout rather than a plain query result.
   207|
   208|Working rule:
   209|- do not stop at listing current pages
   210|- inspect the canonical root as an operating product and judge whether it is decision-ready
   211|
   212|Audit sequence:
   213|1. Read `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`, `SCHEMA.md`, and the latest run manifest under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/`.
   214|2. Check whether the front page is action-first or merely entity-first.
   215|3. Count whether `queries/` and `comparisons/` are populated; if they are empty, call that out explicitly as a leadership-surface gap.
   216|4. Cross-check latest Slack/Trello refresh evidence against derived page timestamps to detect summary lag.
   217|5. Check for migration/hygiene debt in canonical pages:
   218|   - absolute `/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw` path leakage
   219|   - frontmatter/type mistakes
   220|   - stale timestamps after successful refresh

    16|  - live: crontab -l (2026-04-14)
    17|---
    18|
    19|# 2026-04-14 company wiki / skill / cron redefinition
    20|
    21|## Situation
    22|canonical `company-wiki` 자체는 이미 Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion을 한 루트로 묶는 방향으로 정리돼 있다. 문제는 설명면이 완전히 따라오지 못한 점이었다. 특히 `slack-org-wiki`가 source-adapter보다 더 큰 위키처럼 읽힐 여지가 있었고, `create_slack_cron`은 아직도 ZeroClaw-era delivery mental model을 강하게 남기고 있었다.
    23|
    24|## Evidence snapshot
    25|- `SCHEMA.md`와 `index.md`는 이미 canonical root + executive-first reading order를 반영한다.
    26|- latest run manifest `20260414T041702Z.json` 기준 Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/Notion은 모두 source entry를 갖고 있다.
    27|- 같은 manifest에서 Slack admin/oversight inventory는 `missing_scope` / `not_allowed_token_type`로 막혀 있다. 즉 verified baseline은 bot-accessible public-channel inventory/history 일부다.
    28|- system crontab에는 `17 * * * * ... run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`가 살아 있다. 이건 사람에게 보내는 delivery cron이 아니라 canonical substrate refresh cron이다.
    29|
    30|## Analysis
    31|### 1. 위키는 하나로 못 박아야 한다
    32|사용자-facing company memory는 `company-wiki` 하나여야 한다. source별 스킬은 retrieval/diagnostics lane이지, 별도 위키 루트가 아니다.
    33|
    34|### 2. Slack 스킬은 retrieval lane으로 좁혀야 한다
    35|`slack-org-wiki`의 정확한 역할은 auth/bootstrap, inventory, join/history diagnosis, ingestion support다. retained memo나 canonical read surface는 `company-wiki`가 맡는 게 맞다.
    36|
    37|### 3. cron도 둘로 나눠야 한다
    38|- substrate cron: canonical freshness 유지
    39|- delivery cron: 사람에게 Slack 보고/알림 전달
    40|
    41|이 분리가 없으면 운영자가 refresh cron을 delivery surface로 오해하게 된다.
    42|
    43|## Recommendation
    44|- `company-wiki`를 top-level canonical memory surface로 선언 강화
    45|- `slack-org-wiki`를 Slack source-adapter/diagnostic lane으로 재정의
    46|- `create_slack_cron`을 Hermes-first delivery cron skill로 전면 재정의
    47|- `AGENTS.md`에서도 delivery cron vs substrate cron을 분리 명시
    48|
    49|## Result applied in this cycle
    50|- `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`에 canonical ownership, operating modes, cron contract, readiness rubric를 강화했다.
    51|- `workspace/skills/slack-org-wiki.md`를 source-adapter/diagnostic lane 중심으로 재정의했고 routing decision table과 verified operating reality를 추가했다.
    52|- `workspace/skills/create_slack_cron.md`를 Hermes-first delivery cron skill로 재작성했다.
    53|- `workspace/AGENTS.md`에서 `create_slack_cron` 설명을 delivery-cron surface로 수정하고, Slack org/wiki invariants에 substrate-vs-delivery 구분을 추가했다.
    54|", "total_lines": 53, "file_size": 3326, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

**Exploring collaboration tools**

I'm thinking about how I might need more concept pages for organizing tasks and ideas, and I'm considering using tools like Trello, Slack, or Notion. Each has its strengths for collaboration and brainstorming, but I need to figure out which would work best for my needs. I'll look into their features a bit more to assess what fits. It's exciting to think about improving organization and communication!
